page: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a high-density virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 80 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes hyper-threading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI. The instance is provisioned with 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

For storage-intensive operations, the instance includes 9449 GB of local disk storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. In terms of performance, the AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier results in CPU-bound tasks. Benchmark data indicates that the single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance falls within the top 10% of tested systems. This high-compute capability is mirrored in compression and decompression tasks, where single-threaded gzip compression reaches 41.4 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves 999.55 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D160alds_v7 is its low memory density relative to its massive compute footprint. While it provides exceptional raw processing power and substantial local storage, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU limits its suitability for memory-bound applications. Consequently, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local storage but have modest memory footprints, such as high-throughput batch processing, compute-intensive simulations, compression pipelines, and localized database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 160 dedicated vCPUs and 80 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 320.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 9449 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with outstanding compression and decompression throughput. Due to its high compute density and substantial local storage, but relatively low memory allocation, the D160alds_v7 is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, batch processing, and storage-heavy applications that do not require massive memory footprints.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a compute-dense instance featuring 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 features 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-compute, low-memory virtual server featuring 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs and massive local storage for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 160 dedicated vCPUs at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 320.0 GB of RAM and 9449 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU
Best suited for compute-intensive tasks, batch processing, and compression pipelines
